scenario.summary: Q4 budget-to-actual variance analysis — revenue shortfall, COGS overrun, SG&A savings
scenario.situation: It's late November. The FP&A Analyst is building the Q4 variance analysis for the CFO's board package. October actuals are in, and November is trending. Precision Components missed revenue targets by 4.2% due to delayed aerospace orders. COGS is running 6.1% over budget across all divisions — driven by the raw material spike that hit in October. The one bright spot: Automation Systems SG&A is 3.8% under budget thanks to the headcount freeze. The analyst needs to decompose variances by division, identify root causes, and draft the variance commentary before the December board meeting.
dataInputs: Q4 budget by division and cost center
Prior year Q4 actuals for trend comparison
agentSteps: Pulls Q4 budget vs. actuals by division and line item — identifies 14 material variances exceeding 3% threshold
Decomposes Precision Components revenue shortfall: $5.2M volume variance (delayed aerospace orders) + $1.1M price variance (competitive discounting)
Traces COGS overrun to raw material spike: steel +8%, alloys +5.2% — quantifies $12.4M unfavorable purchase price variance across divisions
Identifies favorable SG&A variance in Automation Systems: $2.8M from headcount freeze + $0.9M from deferred consulting spend
Cross-references with Financial Forecasting agent's Q4 v2 forecast — validates that variance drivers are consistent with forecast assumptions
Generates variance commentary with waterfall chart data — revenue walk, margin bridge, and division-level explanations
Flags 3 variances requiring management action: Precision Components backlog recovery, raw material hedging strategy, Specialty Materials overtime trend
scenario.expectedOutput: Complete Q4 variance package: division-level P&L variance analysis, root cause decomposition for all material variances, variance commentary narrative, waterfall chart data, and 3 action items for CFO review

variances.explanation: Delayed aerospace orders ($5.2M volume) + competitive discounting ($1.1M price) + FX impact ($0.8M)
Raw material spike: steel +8% ($12.4M purchase price variance), partially offset by volume decline
Slight delay in Q4 project milestones — expected to catch up in December
Headcount freeze savings ($2.8M) + deferred consulting spend ($0.9M)
Within tolerance — minor timing difference on two contracts
Alloy price increase +5.2% ($6.1M) + overtime costs from expedited orders ($2.9M)
